message:
test(fcp-core): pin ConnectorBinaryTransmissionInfo serde shape (flywheel_connectors-mrnib)

Bead asks for ConnectorBundleSize serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip. fcp-core
has no type literally named ConnectorBundleSize; the closest "connector
bundle size descriptor" is ConnectorBinaryTransmissionInfo
(connector_artifacts.rs:112) — the portable transmission descriptor
mirroring the symbol-layer OTI fields with 6 fields:
- transfer_length: u64 (total object size in bytes)
- symbol_size: u16
- source_blocks: u8
- sub_blocks: u16
- alignment: u8
- payload_hash: Option<[u8; 32]> (with skip_serializing_if + default)

Used in fixtures across signed_package_catalog_serde_roundtrip.rs and
connector_bundle_serde_extended.rs but NOT yet pinned for its own
serde shape.

Coverage:
- 6-field JSON shape pinned when payload_hash is Some
- payload_hash omitted from wire form when None via
  skip_serializing_if (exactly 5 fields appear)
- payload_hash defaults to None when missing from wire form via
  #[serde(default)]
- JSON + CBOR round-trip preserves all fields with payload_hash
  Some and None
- Boundary values per numeric field round-trip (transfer_length 0
  and u64::MAX, symbol_size + sub_blocks at u16::MAX, source_blocks
  + alignment at u8::MAX, all-zero combination)
- payload_hash JSON form is array of 32 numbers — distinct from
  the hex-string form used by ObjectId/Signature elsewhere via
  hex_or_bytes serde adapter (pinned loud since operators reading
  the wire form MUST know to expect an array, not a string)
- payload_hash CBOR round-trips byte-for-byte
- Cross-format consistency: JSON and CBOR decode to same value
- Nested usage in ConnectorBinarySymbolSet preserved through both
  JSON and CBOR round-trip with payload_hash Some and None
- Distinct transfer_length and Some-vs-None payload_hash produce
  distinct wire serializations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
